text.title: Agent Rollback Button Design Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every rollback-button spec enforces. Hard rules — operator input cannot relax them.

rule.statement: Rollback reverts a named bundle (prompt SHA + schema version + tool registry SHA + model name + eval-set version) atomically. Partial-field rollback is forbidden.

rationale.source: Datadog harness-first agents, 2026; SRE rollback patterns

rule.rationale: Partial rollback leaves the system in a state never tested in eval. Atomic bundles are the only reproducible target.

rule.statement: Customer-state fields (conversation history, account artefacts, billing events, audit log) are explicitly listed and remain untouched by rollback. Loss of these fields is a sev-1 incident.

rationale.source: data-loss best practices; GDPR / SOC2 requirements

rule.rationale: Customer messages are user-generated content; deleting them on rollback is a data-loss bug masquerading as a feature.

rule.statement: The rollback target must pass the frozen golden eval at ≥ baseline pass-rate (within the 95% CI of the originally-deployed version). Rollback to a worse-scoring bundle is blocked unless overridden by a named human.

rationale.source: ML reproducibility / Pineau et al. 2020 Reproducibility Checklist

rule.rationale: Rolling forward to a known-bad bundle is the rollback equivalent of "fixing" by reintroducing the bug.

rule.statement: Operators see exactly one button per environment. Multiple "rollback to vX" buttons fragment cognitive load and produce wrong-version selections in incidents.

rationale.source: UX of ops tooling; Google SRE workbook

rule.rationale: Single-button rollback with a clear default and an "advanced" override is more reliable under stress than a menu of versions.

rule.statement: Every rollback emits an audit-log entry: {who, when, from_bundle, to_bundle, eval_score_before, eval_score_after, reason}. Log is append-only and immutable.

rationale.source: SOC2 change-management requirements; incident postmortem practice

rule.rationale: Without audit trail the team relitigates "was that rollback intentional?" forever; insurers require it.

example.title: Atomic bundle definition
example.code: bundle: version: v3.1.0 prompt_sha: a3f1c9 schema_version: 2.0.0 tool_registry_sha: b2d4e1 model: claude-sonnet-4 eval_set_version: golden-v3-frozen-2026-05-15 immutable_fields: [conversations.messages, accounts.*, audit_log.*]
example.note: Everything reverts together; customer state is named as preserved.

example.title: Per-field rollback
example.code: rollback: revert prompt only, keep schema v2.0.0
example.note: Untested combination of prompt v3.0.0 + schema v2.0.0. Eval set never saw this mix.
